core_mechanism: A decomposability criterion for a multivariate Gaussian object specified entirely by its second-moment (covariance) matrix: apply a linear sign-reversing involution to one block of variables and require the resulting matrix to stay positive-semidefinite, a condition that is necessary but (as the paper shows) not sufficient for decomposing the object into a convex mixture of products above the lowest dimension
brief_reason: The abstract characterizes both separability and positivity of the partial transpose purely in terms of the covariance matrix, and shows this sign-flip positivity condition implies decomposition only in the smallest (one-plus-many) case, failing via explicit higher-dimensional counterexamples.
